title: Henckelia cinerea
intro: Henckelia cinerea, also known as the 'Grey Leaf Henckelia', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and belongs to the family Gesneriaceae. The leaves and roots of Henckelia cinerea are used in traditional medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Henckelia cinerea include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional remedies for wound healing, fever reduction, and immune system support.
taxonomy: Henckelia cinerea is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violets or the Gesneriad family.
